question_text: Which of these will produce a larger moment on a door?

options.label: A
options.text: A small force applied near the hinge

options.label: B
options.text: A small force applied far from the hinge

options.label: C
options.text: A large force applied very close to the hinge

options.label: D
options.text: The distance from the hinge doesn't matter

correct_answer: A small force applied far from the hinge
explanation: Moment (turning effect) depends on both the force applied and the distance from the pivot (hinge). The further away from the hinge a force is applied, the larger the moment produced. This is why door handles are positioned far from hinges - a small push near the handle creates a large turning effect. The moment equation is: moment = force × perpendicular distance from pivot.
